text.content:
This set features a spectacular array of fireworks and other commonly available explosives, often arrayed in elaborate formations of traps and delaying measures while the gang conducts its activities. When this set is selected, add 1 Grenade (Improvised) to the inventory of each Vampyre in the scene.

For each performer in the scene, select one object which has been Rigged To Blow. These objects can range from lunchboxes to entire desks, but their appearance is obvious and has been dressed up to showcase their destructive potential.

Mook Options:

  • 1 Point, at the start of a Vampyre's turn: The Vampyre detonates a jerryrigged explosive beneath their feet or at their back, propelling them haphazardly across the room. In place of any movement or action, the Vampyre makes a DV15 technical check, while anyone adjacent to the Vampyre makes a reflex save (roll 1d10 - a result less or equal to the character's Reflex stat passes; use 1/2 physical for NPCs.) The Vampyre then moves up to 20 spaces/40 meters in any direction, ignoring vertical movement. Anyone who fails the Reflex save takes 4d6 damage. If the Vampyre fails their technical check, they also take this damage, are knocked prone on landing, and take an additional 1d6 damage (ignoring armor) for every 10 meters they moved.

  • 2 Points, as a Vampyre's action: one object which has been Rigged To Blow detonates - the object is destroyed and an Armor-Piercing grenade centered on the object's location is triggered. Then, roll 1d10; on a 6+, a Grenade (Improvised) is triggered just outside the radius of the initial explosion, in a random direction. If this effect succeeds, roll another 1d10; on a 10, the secondary effect repeats.

  • 2 Points, during a Vampyre's turn: A curtain falls or other illusions are removed, revealing another object which has been Rigged to Blow. It cannot be detonated until the same time in the next round, at earliest.

  • 3 Points, during a Showstopper or Master of Ceremonies turn, in place of that mook's action: A collection of fireworks and explosives is set off elsewhere, raining down on the scene in a haphazard "distraction" spectacle. At 5 random locations in the scene, a Grenade (Improvised) is detonated.

To choose random directions, roll 1d8, corresponding as follows:

  1. North

  2. Northeast

  3. East

  4. Southeast

  5. South

  6. Southwest

  7. West

  8. Northwest
